creator: portmaster@bsdforge.com
text:
> +--On 23 janvier 2014 13:47:35 -0800 C Hutchinson <portmaster@bsdforge.com>
> wrote:
> | Hello, again.
>
> The problem is that you're not using pkg but the old deprecated
> pkg_install. I've tried to fix the port installation, but pkg_install seems
> to be doing stupid things when creating a port in some case, and we're not
> doing to fix that.
>
> My fix for you would be to install /usr/ports/ports-mgmt/pkg, run pkg2ng,
> add WITH_PKGNG=yes in your /etc/make.conf and it'll work like a charm.
>
> --
> Mathieu Arnold

Hello.
As to my using pkg_install; I'm /not/ using it. I didn't ask for it. If
it's in use, it is because FreeBSD insists I use it, and installs it for
me, without asking.

I only attempted this second fresh install, in the hopes of helping you
resolve this issue. I had no prior notice you had already written it off
as /my/ fault.

In an effort to better isolate the problem, I installed some 350 ports
prior to attempting to install dns/yadifa. None of the other ports had
failures. I spanned the entire port categories, including meta-ports,
and make config-recursive. So, to me, it is clear that 1) it is a
problem with the way you've implemented the port, and 2) it is possible
to implement, without the failure of install. As the previous 350 successful
installed ports indicate.

Sorry, for going off like this. But I have an extremely large workload,
and went out of my way to help you with this issue. Discovering that
I've simply wasted my efforts, doesn't come easy.

As to your solution; I appreciate your suggestion. But going that route
would require installing, maintaining, learning another port that I
don't need. Since it's only intent is to install dns/yadifa. But, as I
noted earlier in this thread; passing -DNO_STAGE solves the problem
w/o the overhead of additional port maintenance.

Thanks for taking the time to respond.

--Chris

>
